rulings.Case Title: FARHAD S MAHJOURI VS. PROVIDENT LIFE AND ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 28, 2008 LINE 11. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT: The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The Court takes judicial notice of the California Settlement Agreement and the Decision and Order of Insurance Commissioner Upon Settlement. The record reflects that the Commissioner has reviewed and withdrawn the subject policy and as a consequence, the relief plaintiff seeks already has been obtained. There is no authority to order the retroactive withdrawal of a policy. Peterson v. American Life & Health Ins. Co. (1995) 48 F.3d 404, 411. With respect to the duty of the Commissioner to oversee claims practices, such acts involve discretionary conduct (Ins. Code sections 790.05 and 790.035) and the Commissioner does not adjudicate claims between insurers and insureds. (Ins. Code section 12921.4(a)). =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SN SANDS CORPORATION DBA S&S TRUCKING VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Denial Of Stay [Ccp 1110b]
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 28, 2008 LINE 17. PLAINTIFF S.N. SANDS CORPORATION'S, D.B.A. S&S TRUCKING, Motion For Denial Of Stay [C.C.P. SECTION 1110b]: HEARING REQUIRED. THE ISSUE IS THE HARM TO PLAINTIFF NOT THIRD PARTIES. GIVEN THAT, THE CITY IS REFUSING TO ACKNOWLEDGE THAT IT WOULD BE RESPONSIBLE FOR ALL CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES INCURRED BY PLAINTIFF, THE EQUITABLE CONCERNS STRONGLY FAVOR PLAINTIFF. =(302/PJM/VC)
